name: Reynald Jemane
bio:
Physical Appearance: Breton male wearing a blue and green outfit with doeskin shoes. Has an identical twin brother, Guilbert. Wields a shortsword of high quality and carries spare tan linens. Always has four bottles of ale on his person.
Race: Breton
Personality Keywords: warrior, alcoholic, separated twin, lazy, drinker, lost, reunited, changed
Role: Reynald Jemane is a warrior living in Chorrol who spends most of his time in an ale-induced haze at The Grey Mare. He has an identical twin brother, Guilbert, from whom he was separated at a very young age when their home was attacked by ogres. Reynald was found in the wilderness by traveling merchants and raised at Weynon Priory, knowing nothing of his brother's survival. The two are eventually reunited and together reclaim their childhood home at Weatherleah. He carries his house key, four bottles of ale, spare clothing, and gold.
Home Location Information: Initially owns a house in Chorrol but rarely uses it. Later moves to Weatherleah with his brother Guilbert after reclaiming their family home.
Daily Routine:
Before reunion: Reynald sleeps from midnight to 6am. He unlocks his door and lazily wanders upstairs until 8am, when he locks the door and heads to The Grey Mare. He drinks for sixteen hours straight until midnight, then returns home to sleep.
After moving to Weatherleah: He begins his day at 6am by going outside to wander until 10am, when he comes inside for breakfast. At noon he goes back outside to wander for 12 hours. At midnight he tries to sleep, but as there's only one bed in the house, he may end up pacing aimlessly if Guilbert gets there first.
Personality - The Lost Brother: Reynald has spent years drowning himself in ale, perhaps trying to fill the void left by his traumatic childhood and unknown family. Unlike his sober twin Guilbert who lives a stable life in Cheydinhal, Reynald has been lost in drink, spending virtually all his waking hours at The Grey Mare. His lazy lifestyle and constant drinking suggest someone who hasn't found purpose or direction in life. However, the reunion with his brother and the recovery of their family home at Weatherleah marks a turning point - his new routine shows him spending more time outdoors and less time drinking, suggesting the beginning of a healthier life. The fact that he and Guilbert sometimes compete for the single bed at Weatherleah adds a touch of normalcy to their rediscovered brotherhood, showing they're learning to live together again after years apart.
author: Bethesda
